# Bukas Cafe

> Bukas Cafe is a Filipino progressive cafe at 56-14 Van Horn St, Elmhurst, Queens, NY 11373. Owned by Elmhurst locals Anna Javier and Angel Carreon, it serves progressive Filipino drinks and comfort food — signature lattes like the Bukas Na (salted cream, asin tibuok) and Ube Eclipse, an espresso series called Clouds Over MNL, Japan-grown matcha milled in the Philippines, and kitchen items like the Adobo Jalapeño Melt. "Bukas" means both "tomorrow" and "open" in Tagalog. Open Wednesday–Sunday from 9:00 AM (until 3:00 PM most days, 6:00 PM on Fridays), closed Monday and Tuesday. Dine-in and takeout, no delivery, typically $10–20 per person. Phone: (929) 781-4080. Email: hello@bukascafe.com. Instagram: @bukas.cafe. Ranked the #1 new NYC coffee & tea spot of 2026 on Beli.

## Pages

- [Home](https://bukascafe.com/): Story, signature menu highlights, reviews, press, location and hours
- [Menu](https://bukascafe.com/menu.html): Full menu — espresso, signature lattes, tea, matcha, non-coffee drinks, food, and add-ons with prices
- [Events](https://bukascafe.com/events.html): Pop-ups and community events, including collabs and after-hours nights

## Press

- [The Infatuation review](https://www.theinfatuation.com/new-york/reviews/bukas-cafe): "That little savory touch makes the drinks pop" (March 2026)
- [NBC New York feature](https://www.nbcnewyork.com/video/queens/bukas-cafe-how-this-filipino-coffee-shop-captured-the-heart-of-queens/6507705/): "How this Filipino coffee shop captured the heart of Queens" (2026)
- [Eater NY](https://ny.eater.com/news/409052/bukas-cafe-opens-filipino-restaurant-nyc-queens-rare-salts): "Bukas Cafe opens with a modern Filipino food and coffee menu in Elmhurst" (February 2026)
- [QNS feature](https://qns.com/2026/02/bukas-cafe-filipino-dishes-modern-twist/): "Bukas Cafe serves Filipino dishes with a modern twist" (February 2026)
